Voegt determineEndWinner() toe die na een end bepaalt welke partij wint
en hoeveel stenen scoren. Regels:
- Alleen stenen binnen de house (center <= buitenste ring + steenradius) tellen

endResult wordt ingesteld bij transitie naar SCORING state (niet in draw loop)
zodat het exact eenmalig per end wordt berekend. Score-update naar

commit.message:
fix(package-filter): fix O(n²) complexity in cleanupDistFiles (#5797)

* fix(package-filter): fix O(n²) complexity in cleanupDistFiles

The previous implementation used Array.find() inside a forEach loop,
resulting in O(n²) time complexity: for each _distfiles entry, it scanned
all manifest.versions to find a matching tarball URL.

For packages with thousands of versions (e.g. @graphql-codegen/* with
5000-6000 versions), this caused severe performance degradation:
- Blocked the Node.js event loop on every metadata request
- Caused liveness probe timeouts and pod restarts under load
- Real-world impact: npm install taking 17+ minutes instead of ~5 minutes

Fix: build a Set of active tarball URLs in one O(n) pass, then check
each _distfiles entry against the Set in O(1). Total complexity: O(n).

Benchmarked on a real project (survey-editor-frontend):
- Before: 17:54 with original plugin
- After:   7:03 with this fix

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: bypass manual-open check when sun clears obstacle threshold (#49)

* fix: bypass manual-open check when sun clears obstacle threshold

When a cover is in obstacle-blocked (no-sun) mode the cover is raised
to 100%. When the sun then rises above the obstacle threshold, the
manual-open check would see position=100% and refuse to drive the
cover back down, leaving it raised indefinitely.

Fix: track per-cover which entities were obstacle-blocked last cycle
(_obstacle_was_blocking set). On the first cycle after sun clears the
threshold, bypass the manual-open check — same pattern as
_first_facade_hit_this_cycle used for the sunrise case.

* feat(local-log): batching buffer with 500ms timeout flush

Replace serial write-through promise queue with Queue<string> buffer
from @tsdotnet/queue. Events accumulate in memory and flush as a single
appendFile call after FLUSH_DELAY_MS (500ms) of inactivity, or
immediately via flushCurrentLog().

- logEvent() buffers events and schedules a debounced timer
- _flush() drains buffer, writes all lines in one I/O call
- _flushPromise tracks in-flight timer write so flushCurrentLog()
  can await it even after the timer has already fired
- flushCurrentLog() cancels timer, awaits in-flight write, then drains
- resetLocalLogForTest() clears timer, promise, and buffer
